2026-04-21T07:48:38.7636044Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7636457Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7636797Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7637078Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7637320Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7637506Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7637638Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7637789Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7637928Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7638116Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T07:48:38.7638502Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T07:48:38.7638709Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-21T07:52:48.5469376Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T07:49:47.646987625Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58437,\"title\":\"[Azure AI Extensions OpenAI] Docs: Add Skills API documentation and sample\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57909 (commit [REDACTED]) by `@nick863`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57909 (\\u0026#34;Generate code for Session, Blueprint and Skills\\u0026#34;) added new Skills-related types to the `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` package:\\n\\n- `ContainerSkill` — abstract base class for skills (type discriminator)\\n- `ContainerSkillType` — enum with values `Inline` and `SkillReference`\\n- `ResponsesInlineSkillParam` — defines an inline skill with a name, description, and a base64-encoded zip bundle source (`ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam`)\\n- `ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam` — inline skill payload (base64-encoded zip, media type `application/zip`)\\n- `ResponsesSkillReferenceParam` — references an already-published skill by ID with an optional version\\n- `LocalSkillParam` — local skill definition (name, description, path to directory)\\n\\nThese types allow callers to provide custom skills directly in Responses API requests, either inline or by referencing a deployed skill.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG is incomplete**: `2.1.0-beta.1` only says \\u0026#34;The sample for Hosted agent was updated.\\u0026#34; The new Skills APIs are not mentioned at all.\\n- **README has no Skills section**: The README Table of Contents and Examples section cover many tools (Web Search, Bing, MCP, OpenAPI, etc.) but has no entry for Skills.\\n- **No sample file**: There is no `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/samples/` file covering Skills.\\n- **No test-backed snippet**: There is no `tests/Samples/Sample_Skills.cs` (or equivalent) with `#region Snippet:` tags wired to a README placeholder.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the sparse 2.1.0-beta.1 entry with a richer one:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (2026-04-21)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added Skills support for the Responses API. Use `ResponsesInlineSkillParam` to embed a skill inline (as a base64-encoded zip bundle) or `ResponsesSkillReferenceParam` to reference a pre-published skill by ID. Local skill directories can be described with `LocalSkillParam`.\\n- The sample for Hosted agent was updated.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Add Skills section to README Table of Contents\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — in the Table of Contents, add an entry after `Memory search tool` and before `Azure Function tool`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n  - [Skills](#skills)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Write the sample test file\\n\\n**Create:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/tests/Samples/Sample_Skills.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.IO;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing OpenAI;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class ExtensionsOpenAISamples : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        public void SkillReference()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:SkillReference\\n            ProjectOpenAIClient projectClient = new(\\n                endpoint: new Uri(TestEnvironment.ProjectEndpoint),\\n                tokenProvider: new Azure.Identity.DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            var responsesClient = projectClient.GetProjectResponsesClient();\\n\\n            // Reference a pre-published skill by its ID (and optionally a version).\\n            var skillRef = new ResponsesSkillReferenceParam(skillId: \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)\\n            {\\n                Version = \\u0026#34;latest\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n\\n            var options = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponseCreationOptions();\\n            options.Tools.Add(skillRef);\\n\\n            var response = responsesClient.CreateResponse(\\n                model: \\u0026#34;gpt-4o\\u0026#34;,\\n                input: \\u0026#34;Use the skill to answer: what is the capital of France?\\u0026#34;,\\n                options: options);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine(response.Value.OutputText);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        public void InlineSkill()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:InlineSkill\\n            ProjectOpenAIClient projectClient = new(\\n                endpoint: new Uri(TestEnvironment.ProjectEndpoint),\\n                tokenProvider: new Azure.Identity.DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            var responsesClient = projectClient.GetProjectResponsesClient();\\n\\n            // Read a zip bundle that contains the skill implementation.\\n            byte[] skillZipBytes = File.ReadAllBytes(\\u0026#34;path/to/skill.zip\\u0026#34;);\\n            string base64Zip = Convert.ToBase64String(skillZipBytes);\\n\\n            var inlineSkill = new ResponsesInlineSkillParam(\\n                name: \\u0026#34;my-inline-skill\\u0026#34;,\\n                description: \\u0026#34;A custom skill embedded inline\\u0026#34;,\\n                source: new ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam(data: base64Zip));\\n\\n            var options = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponseCreationOptions();\\n            options.Tools.Add(inlineSkill);\\n\\n            var response = responsesClient.CreateResponse(\\n                model: \\u0026#34;gpt-4o\\u0026#34;,\\n                input: \\u0026#34;Use the skill to process my request.\\u0026#34;,\\n                options: options);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine(response.Value.OutputText);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### 2b. Add README snippet placeholders\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add the following **Skills** section after the `Memory search tool` section and before the `Azure Function tool` section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Skills\\n\\nSkills are reusable capabilities that can be attached to a Responses API request either by **referencing** a pre-published skill or by providing the skill **inline** as a zip bundle.\\n\\n#### Reference a published skill\\n\\n```C# Snippet:SkillReference\\n```\\n\\n#### Embed a skill inline\\n\\n```C# Snippet:InlineSkill\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Skills** entry is missing from the Examples subsection and needs to be added as described above.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — injects snippet code from the new test file into README placeholders. Verify the README diff shows the Skills code blocks populated.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — regenerates public API listing files (not needed here since no new public API was added, but run to confirm).\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T07:50:12.947134826Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":10,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4301100346,\"number\":58437,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Azure AI Extensions OpenAI] Docs: Add Skills API documentation and sample\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57909 (commit [REDACTED]) by `@nick863`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57909 (\\\"Generate code for Session, Blueprint and Skills\\\") added new Skills-related types to the `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` package:\\n\\n- `ContainerSkill` — abstract base class for skills (type discriminator)\\n- `ContainerSkillType` — enum with values `Inline` and `SkillReference`\\n- `ResponsesInlineSkillParam` — defines an inline skill with a name, description, and a base64-encoded zip bundle source (`ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam`)\\n- `ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam` — inline skill payload (base64-encoded zip, media type `application/zip`)\\n- `ResponsesSkillReferenceParam` — references an already-published skill by ID with an optional version\\n- `LocalSkillParam` — local skill definition (name, description, path to directory)\\n\\nThese types allow callers to provide custom skills directly in Responses API requests, either inline or by referencing a deployed skill.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG is incomplete**: `2.1.0-beta.1` only says \\\"The sample for Hosted agent was updated.\\\" The new Skills APIs are not mentioned at all.\\n- **README has no Skills section**: The README Table of Contents and Examples section cover many tools (Web Search, Bing, MCP, OpenAPI, etc.) but has no entry for Skills.\\n- **No sample file**: There is no `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/samples/` file covering Skills.\\n- **No test-backed snippet**: There is no `tests/Samples/Sample_Skills.cs` (or equivalent) with `#region Snippet:` tags wired to a README placeholder.\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the sparse 2.1.0-beta.1 entry with a richer one:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (2026-04-21)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added Skills support for the Responses API. Use `ResponsesInlineSkillParam` to embed a skill inline (as a base64-encoded zip bundle) or `ResponsesSkillReferenceParam` to reference a pre-published skill by ID. Local skill directories can be described with `LocalSkillParam`.\\n- The sample for Hosted agent was updated.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Add Skills section to README Table of Contents\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — in the Table of Contents, add an entry after `Memory search tool` and before `Azure Function tool`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n  - [Skills](#skills)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Write the sample test file\\n\\n**Create:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/tests/Samples/Sample_Skills.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.IO;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\nusing OpenAI;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class ExtensionsOpenAISamples : SamplesBase\\u003cProjectsOpenAITestEnvironment\\u003e\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        public void SkillReference()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:SkillReference\\n            ProjectOpenAIClient projectClient = new(\\n                endpoint: new Uri(TestEnvironment.ProjectEndpoint),\\n                tokenProvider: new Azure.Identity.DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            var responsesClient = projectClient.GetProjectResponsesClient();\\n\\n            // Reference a pre-published skill by its ID (and optionally a version).\\n            var skillRef = new ResponsesSkillReferenceParam(skillId: \\\"\\u003cyour-skill-id\\u003e\\\")\\n            {\\n                Version = \\\"latest\\\"\\n            };\\n\\n            var options = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponseCreationOptions();\\n            options.Tools.Add(skillRef);\\n\\n            var response = responsesClient.CreateResponse(\\n                model: \\\"gpt-4o\\\",\\n                input: \\\"Use the skill to answer: what is the capital of France?\\\",\\n                options: options);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine(response.Value.OutputText);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        public void InlineSkill()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:InlineSkill\\n            ProjectOpenAIClient projectClient = new(\\n                endpoint: new Uri(TestEnvironment.ProjectEndpoint),\\n                tokenProvider: new Azure.Identity.DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            var responsesClient = projectClient.GetProjectResponsesClient();\\n\\n            // Read a zip bundle that contains the skill implementation.\\n            byte[] skillZipBytes = File.ReadAllBytes(\\\"path/to/skill.zip\\\");\\n            string base64Zip = Convert.ToBase64String(skillZipBytes);\\n\\n            var inlineSkill = new ResponsesInlineSkillParam(\\n                name: \\\"my-inline-skill\\\",\\n                description: \\\"A custom skill embedded inline\\\",\\n                source: new ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam(data: base64Zip));\\n\\n            var options = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponseCreationOptions();\\n            options.Tools.Add(inlineSkill);\\n\\n            var response = responsesClient.CreateResponse(\\n                model: \\\"gpt-4o\\\",\\n                input: \\\"Use the skill to process my request.\\\",\\n                options: options);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine(response.Value.OutputText);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### 2b. Add README snippet placeholders\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add the following **Skills** section after the `Memory search tool` section and before the `Azure Function tool` section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Skills\\n\\nSkills are reusable capabilities that can be attached to a Responses API request either by **referencing** a pre-published skill or by providing the skill **inline** as a zip bundle.\\n\\n#### Reference a published skill\\n\\n```C# Snippet:SkillReference\\n```\\n\\n#### Embed a skill inline\\n\\n```C# Snippet:InlineSkill\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Skills** entry is missing from the Examples subsection and needs to be added as described above.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — injects snippet code from the new test file into README placeholders. Verify the README diff shows the Skills code blocks populated.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — regenerates public API listing files (not needed here since no new public API was added, but run to confirm).\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u003e\\n\\u003e ```yaml\\n\\u003e network:\\n\\u003e   allowed:\\n\\u003e     - defaults\\n\\u003e     - \\\"pkgs.dev.azure.com\\\"\\n\\u003e     - trace spans and attributes for additional tool call types used in the OpenAI Responses API:\\n\\n- `code_interpreter_call` — captures `code`, `outputs`, `status`\\n- `file_search_call` — captures `queries`, `results`, `status`\\n- `web_search_call` — captures `action`, `status`\\n- `mcp_call` / `mcp_*` variants — captures `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, `status`\\n\\nNew telemetry test
